response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine developer-tooling interest — including prior IDE work on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack does not match. The role requires strong Java and Kotlin expertise with deep JVM understanding plus solid Spring ecosystem experience; the resume shows a career centered on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and C/C++, with Java appearing only peripherally and no Kotlin, Spring, or JVM-internals work at all. Without the required JVM/enterprise-framework foundation, the fit for this specific position is weak despite otherwise strong engineering fundamentals.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong developer-tooling track record (test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, large-scale low-latency systems
concerns: No Kotlin and no meaningful Spring/enterprise-JVM framework experience
Java is a minor, dated skill rather than the strong expertise the role requires
Recent experience is TypeScript/Node-centric, far from JVM tooling and application servers
